parentQuestion: In anatomical position, the ulna is ___ to the radius.

scaffolds.q: What does 'medial' mean in anatomical terms?
scaffolds.explain: Medial means toward the midline (center) of your body. Lateral means away from the midline. Think: the nose is medial to the eyes. Your pinky is medial to your thumb. Simple, elegant, precise.

scaffolds.q: In anatomical position, which bone is on the thumb side of the forearm?
scaffolds.explain: The radius is on the lateral (thumb) side of the forearm. The ulna is on the medial (pinky) side. The humerus is the upper arm bone — wrong neighborhood entirely. Let me guide you to the right location.
